section.heading: Amounts obligated but unspent; prohibition on use of funds

subsection.heading: Amounts obligated but unspent
subsection.chapeau: Any county funds that were obligated by the applicable participating county before October 1, 2017, but are unspent on October 1, 2020—

paragraph.content: may, at the option of the participating county, be deemed to have been reserved by the participating county on October 1, 2020, for expenditure in accordance with this subchapter; and

subparagraph.content: may be used by the participating county for any authorized use under section 7142(a) of this title; and
on a determination by the participating county under subparagraph (A) to use the county funds, shall be available for projects initiated after October 1, 2020, subject to section 7144 of this title.

content.p: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, effective beginning on November 15, 2021, no county funds made available under this subchapter may be used by any participating county for any lobbying activity, regardless of the purpose for which the funds are obligated on or before that date.

section.sourceCredit: (Pub. L. 106–393, title III, § 304, as added Pub. L. 117–58, div. D, title XII, § 41202(g)(2), Nov. 15, 2021, 135 Stat. 1134.)

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 304 of Pub. L. 106–393 was renumbered section 305 and is classified to section 7144 of this title.

note.heading: Wage Rate Requirements
note.p: For provisions relating to rates of wages to be paid to laborers and mechanics on projects for construction, alteration, or repair work funded under div. D or an amendment by div. D of Pub. L. 117–58, including authority of Secretary of Labor, see section 18851 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare.
